changes_made: Added k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_egress to ensure comprehensive access control coverage
review_notes: The functions provided are mostly accurate and well-named, mapping well to Kubernetes security controls related to access control. However, to fully align with the HIPAA access control requirements, it's beneficial to include both ingress and egress network policies. Naming conventions are followed correctly, and the functions are descriptive and specific.
title: 164_312_a_1: 164.312(a)(1) Access control
step1_reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.312(a)(1) focuses on access control, which is directly applicable to Kubernetes through RBAC and network policies. RBAC in Kubernetes can enforce least privilege by ensuring no excessive permissions are granted (e.g., avoiding cluster-admin roles) and by managing service account token usage. NetworkPolicies can be used to restrict network access, providing an additional layer of access control.
